what_it_does: This file is an empty Git placeholder named .gitkeep in the runtime directory; it contains no code, configuration, or data and exists solely so Git tracks the directory.
observations: The file has zero bytes of content.
No symbols, comments, or executable logic are present.
The filename and path indicate it is a directory-preservation marker.
notes: Empty Git directory placeholder for runtime; nothing to review beyond its path.
purpose_vs_actual: The implied purpose—keeping the runtime directory tracked by Git—matches the actual content, which is an empty placeholder file.
intention_signals: Reserve the runtime directory as a persistent location in the working tree so it survives checkouts and clones even before any runtime artifacts are generated.
contribution: It contributes a stable, version-controlled path for the runtime directory, ensuring downstream tooling or deployments can rely on the directory existing without needing to create it dynamically.
summary_for_embedding: An empty .gitkeep file at company/runtime/.gitkeep that keeps the runtime directory tracked by Git.
intention_for_embedding: To ensure the runtime directory exists in the repository checkout even when it has no other contents.
